item.title: The Morning Show: Australia's no. 1 Morning TV Show - Channel 7 | 7NEWS
item.description: The Morning Show with Larry Emdur and Kylie Gillies is the latest news, lifestyle, recipes, entertainment and weather. The Morning Show brings you a mix of news, entertainment, lifestyle and human interest stories - Weekdays from 9:00am across Australia.

item.title: Sunrise: Australia's No. 1 Breakfast Show - Channel 7 | 7NEWS
item.description: Sunrise with Natalie Barr, Matt Shirvington, Edwina Bartholomew, Sam Mac and Mark Beretta is the latest news, sport, entertainment and weather. Win with the Cash Cow. Australia's most popular brekky show - Weekdays from 5:30am, weekends from 7am on Channel 7.

item.title: Iran, Israel and US war live updates: US President Donald Trump says five Iranian National Women’s Soccer Team players reportedly granted asylum in Australia
item.description: Follow along as we cover all the breaking news as the conflict between Iran, the United States and Israel escalates.

item.title: 2026 Ineos Grenadier review: Quick drive
item.description: Could the death of the V8 Toyota LandCruiser 70 Series spell an uptick in popularity for the rugged, recently updated Ineos Grenadier?

item.title: Lululemon hit with $702,000 fine for breaching consumer law
item.description: Thousands of Aussie customers have been impacted by the company’s actions.

item.title: Alastair Clarkson on the brink of failure at North Melbourne ahead of crucial fourth year
item.description: ‘He thinks this will be Alastair’s last year.’

item.title: Melbourne superstar Kysaiah Pickett flies to Northern Territory amid relationship break-up
item.description: There are fears he may miss the first game of the year but the club understands ‘the difficult situation’.

item.title: Learner driver arrested after allegedly firing shots at police during wild car chase
item.description: The pursuit came to a dramatic end as officers closed in on the woman.

item.title: Ineos Grenadier's smaller, electrified SUV sibling back on track
item.description: Ineos says plans for its more compact Fusilier electrified SUV are now "alive and well" after the project was previously put on hold.

item.title: Petrol-powered Ineos Grenadier safe despite slow sales
item.description: Low demand for petrol variants of the Grenadier off-roaders won't stop Ineos Automotive from continuing to offer a choice of engines in Australia.

item.title: Public holiday campaign: NSW Shop, Distributive and Allied Employees Association pushing for extra day off in September
item.description: Advocates say the extra day off would be a win for families and local economies.

item.title: Two more members of Iranian soccer cohort refuse to board plane home
item.description: ‘I had the paperwork ready to execute that immediately.’

item.title: Suzuki Australia: "Safety is paramount" despite ANCAP blows
item.description: Suzuki Australia's general manager says safety remains a top priority in its vehicles, despite recent crash-testing and recall woes.

item.title: Matt Canavan elected new Nationals leader after David Littleproud’s shock resignation
item.description: Darren Chester has been selected as the party’s new deputy leader.

item.title: Six people killed as bus catches fire in Switzerland
item.description: Police believe the fire may have been deliberately lit.

item.title: St Basil’s nursing home fined years after almost half of its residents died from COVID outbreak
item.description: The aged care home was charged with workplace safety breaches after 45 residents died in the outbreak.

item.title: Record numbers of patients leaving NSW hospital emergency departments without treatment
item.description: A medical expert says one state’s healthcare system is in ‘crisis’.

item.title: AFL overhauls ‘outdated’ illicit drugs policy as three-strike system and urine testing gets thrown out
item.description: An AFL great has called the players out after their ‘frustrating’ get-out-of-jail card was torn up.

item.title: Suzuki open to stealing RAV4 PHEV customers Across from Toyota
item.description: Suzuki's local boss hasn't shut the door on the RAV4 PHEV-based Across SUV in Australia, but says global HQ hasn't offered it to him yet...

item.title: Bundaberg floodwaters to peak slightly lower than forecast but could linger for days
item.description: ‘We’re not new to this, we’ve done it before.’

item.title: Gus Lamont search: Police return to South Australian outback station in hopes of finding missing four-year-old boy
item.description: Gus Lamont has not been since September after he vanished from his family’s remote outback station in South Australia.

item.title: Hawthorn star Dylan Moore a ‘shell of a human’ after details of arrest in Arizona become public
item.description: More details have emerged of the bombshell arrest and the ‘tension’ that suddenly followed.

item.title: Dawson’s Creek actor Joshua Jackson breaks silence over co-star James Van Der Beek’s death
item.description: ‘Hits me in a very different way.’

item.title: Students unable to complete NAPLAN assessment after website crashes during first day of testing
item.description: Schools were told to pause testing while the ‘widespread issue’ was investigated.

item.title: Richmond focusing on themselves amid Carlton leaked audio drama ahead of Round 1 clash
item.description: Adem Yze is taking a psychological approach to the Blues after they were shredded by their former captain.

item.title: Experts reveal just when soaring fuel costs will drive up grocery prices across Australia
item.description: Households are expected to pay more within weeks.

item.title: Couple drown at Shellharbour Beach in NSW’s south as bystanders pull pair from the surf after seeing them ‘struggling’
item.description: Police have praised hero bystanders who frantically tried to save their lives.

item.title: Diabetic driver William Swale claims he didn't hear 10 alerts before deadly Royal Daylesford Hotel crash
item.description: The Victorian property tycoon who killed five people ploughing into a pub has made a stunning claim about the moments before the tragedy.
